sentences.en: The historian proposes that what contemporaries condemned as corruption was, viewed structurally, the predictable friction bill of administering an empire whose provinces invented novel shortages faster than any audit could travel.
sentences.zh: 作者主张：时人所斥之贪腐，若从结构着眼，不外是治理广袤帝国时不可避免的摩擦成本——各行省制造短缺的速度，快过任何一道审计所能抵达。

sentences.en: Whether that thesis survives denser archival triangulation remains unsettled, yet its vocabulary alone has already persuaded several neighboring debates to restage their questions.
sentences.zh: 这一论点能否经受更密致的档案检验尚无定论，但其术语本身已促使若干相邻论战改换提问的舞台。
